compiled_state.title: Sub-agent commit workflow must be run inside the sub-agent context, not from orchestrator
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Observed
The sprint per-worktree-review-commit flow requires the orchestrator to: (1) edit files in the sub-agent worktree, (2) dispatch a reviewer that writes reviewer-findings.json to the sub-agent worktree, and (3) commit within the sub-agent worktree. All three steps are blocked by the main-repo isolation hook when executed from the orchestrator session.

Workaround: resume each sub-agent via SendMessage and have it run the full commit workflow itself. This works but doubles token usage (sub-agent runs ~160-250 tool calls just for commit/review).

## Impact
Per-worktree-review-commit cannot be executed by the orchestrator as designed. The orchestrator must delegate commit work back to sub-agents, which was not the intended design and wastes significant context.

## Expected
Either: (a) the per-worktree-review-commit workflow should be built to execute inside the sub-agent context before it returns to the orchestrator, or (b) the isolation hook should be scoped to block only edits to main-branch-tracked files, not all files under REPO_ROOT.

## Fix Direction
Option A (preferred): Sprint sub-agent dispatch prompt should include the commit workflow as the final step before returning the STATUS: report. Sub-agents self-commit before reporting to the orchestrator, eliminating the need for orchestrator-driven per-worktree commit.
Option B: Update per-worktree-review-commit.md to explicitly document that it must run inside the sub-agent context, and update the sprint skill to resume the sub-agent for commits instead of running the commit workflow from the orchestrator.

comments.body: Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present (or pre-loaded triage); intent-aligned assumed. Classification: behavioral cluster, Score: 2 (BASIC). Fix applied: allowlist MAIN_REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/ paths in hook_worktree_edit_guard (pre-bash-functions.sh). Tests: test-worktree-guard-agent-worktree-allowlist.sh (8 pass), test-worktree-agent-paths.sh (6 pass). Anti-pattern scan: no other occurrences found. FIX_RESULT: resolved
comments.author: Test
